sprintGoal: Reduce adoption friction and prevent memory bleed: default writes to scope_agent, cache recall in Redis, add memclaw_session_start warm-context tool, add local embedder fallback, and add memclaw-init CLI.

workItems.objective: Change the default visibility on memclaw_write from None to 'scope_agent', and verify what None currently resolves to in the storage layer so the change is safe.
acceptanceCriteria: memclaw_write called without visibility= produces a memory with visibility='scope_agent' in the DB
Existing memories with explicit visibility= are unaffected
pytest tests/test_mcp_*.py passes (no regressions)
The change is gated: standalone mode only — gateway path already enforces explicit visibility from the caller

reasons: Changes default behavior for existing agents that relied on None falling through to org-level visibility
Requires verifying what None currently resolves to before changing the default

payload.hint: Line 726: visibility param on memclaw_write. Trace None through to storage call to confirm current fallback before changing. Also check memclaw_manage if it has a visibility param.
payload.safe_change: Only change the Python default= value; do not alter storage-layer logic.

workItems.objective: Add Redis-backed caching to memclaw_recall responses. Cache key = agent_id + query hash + top_k. TTL = 5 minutes. Skip cache when cross_context=True or filter_agent_id is set.
acceptanceCriteria: A second identical memclaw_recall call within 5 minutes returns the cached result (verified by log line or latency < 50ms)
cross_context=True bypasses cache
Cache miss path is identical to today's behavior
If Redis is unavailable the tool falls through to the live path — no errors surfaced to caller
pytest tests/test_mcp_recall.py passes (create test file if none exists covering cache hit/miss/bypass)

reasons: Redis already in stack with graceful fallback (cache.py)
Cache is read-only overlay — wrong cache hit degrades result quality but does not corrupt data
5-minute TTL limits blast radius of stale hits

payload.hint: cache.py already has cache_get/cache_set with TTL. The recall tool is memclaw_recall (line ~571). Cache key pattern: 'recall:{agent_id}:{sha256(query+str(top_k))[:12]}'. Serialize the JSON result string directly — it's already a string return.

workItems.objective: Add a memclaw_session_start MCP tool that returns warm context for the calling agent: top-5 memories by recency+weight, active keystone rules, and any procedures with reliability >= 0.6. Designed to be called once at session start and injected into the system prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: memclaw_session_start is listed in the MCP tool registry (19 → 20 tools)
Returns JSON with keys: memories (list), keystones (list), procedures (list)
Respects agent_id scoping — only returns memories the agent can read
pytest tests/test_mcp_session.py passes with at least: tool exists, returns correct structure, respects agent_id
plugin/tools.json and baseline fixtures regenerated and committed
Tool description explicitly states: 'Call once at session start. Paste result into system prompt for zero-latency context.'

reasons: Additive only — new tool, no changes to existing tools
Composes existing memclaw_recall + memclaw_keystones + memclaw_procedure_manage calls internally

payload.hint: Follow the pattern of memclaw_review (new file in tools/, wired into mcp_server.py). The tool should fan out to: (1) memclaw_list top_k=5 sorted by weight desc, (2) memclaw_keystones, (3) memclaw_procedure_manage op=stats or similar. Combine and return as structured JSON.

workItems.objective: Add a local embedding provider that calls an Ollama-compatible /api/embeddings endpoint. Configurable via EMBEDDING_PROVIDER=local, LOCAL_EMBEDDING_URL, and LOCAL_EMBEDDING_MODEL env vars. Falls back gracefully to fake provider if the local endpoint is unreachable at startup.
acceptanceCriteria: EMBEDDING_PROVIDER=local + LOCAL_EMBEDDING_URL=http://localhost:11434/api/embeddings writes memories with real embeddings when Ollama is running
If LOCAL_EMBEDDING_URL is unreachable at startup, a WARNING is logged and provider falls back to FakeEmbeddingProvider (no crash)
pytest tests/test_embedding_local.py passes: mock httpx call returns a 1024-dim vector, embedding stored correctly
config.py has local_embedding_url and local_embedding_model fields with sane defaults
Settings validator warns (not raises) when EMBEDDING_PROVIDER=local and LOCAL_EMBEDDING_URL is unset

reasons: Embedding dimension must match pgvector column (1024d) — Ollama models like nomic-embed-text output 768d by default; must document required model or add dimension validation
New config fields in Settings could break existing .env validation if added incorrectly

payload.hint: config.py line 28 shows embedding_provider='openai' with comment 'fake | openai | local' — the hook is already there. Add LocalEmbeddingProvider in providers/local_provider.py using httpx.AsyncClient. Wire it in common/llm/registry.py (where get_llm_provider and embedding init live). The Ollama embeddings endpoint: POST /api/embeddings {model, prompt}. Dimension warning: document that LOCAL_EMBEDDING_MODEL must output 1024d vectors (mxbai-embed-large, or nomic-embed-text with Matryoshka truncation).

workItems.objective: Add a memclaw-init CLI script that: (1) pings the MemClaw health endpoint, (2) prints a ready-to-paste MCP config block for Claude Code / mcpServers, (3) optionally registers the agent if --agent-id is passed.
acceptanceCriteria: python -m memclaw_init --url http://192.168.1.53:8001 prints the MCP config block and a health status line
python -m memclaw_init --url http://... --agent-id my-agent calls /agents/register and prints confirmation
python -m memclaw_init --url http://unreachable exits with code 1 and a clear error message
Script is a single file, no new dependencies beyond httpx (already in requirements)

reasons: New standalone script with no existing code changed
No framework needed — stdlib argparse + httpx

payload.hint: Create cli/memclaw_init.py. Use httpx.get(url + '/health') for the ping. Print the MCP config as a JSON block the user can paste into ~/.claude/settings.json mcpServers. Registration endpoint: check existing routes for /agents/register or equivalent. Single file, runnable as 'python cli/memclaw_init.py' or 'python -m cli.memclaw_init'. Add a small __main__ self-check that mocks httpx and asserts the config block is valid JSON.

workItems.objective: Write SPRINT-UX-RELIABILITY_SIGNOFF.md summarising outcomes, metrics, lessons learned, and any deliberate deferrals.
acceptanceCriteria: SPRINT-UX-RELIABILITY_SIGNOFF.md exists in sprints/sprint-ux-reliability/
All 5 task outcomes are recorded (completed / failed / notes)
Lessons learned section has at least 2 entries
File is committed to the sprint branch

warnings: UX-04: local embedder dimension must match pgvector column (1024d). nomic-embed-text outputs 768d by default — executor must document or enforce compatible model.
UX-01: None→scope_agent default change is a behavioral break for any agent that intentionally relied on org-wide write fallback. Low risk in standalone/single-tenant but worth noting in signoff.

metrics.notes: UX-01/02/03 are low-effort and can be done sequentially in one sitting. UX-04 and UX-05 are independent and can follow. UX-Z01 gates on all.
